name: Hobbiton
description: The real life Hobbiton, this sheep farm has been forever transformed since being built for The Hobbit film series.

name: Waitomo Glowworm Caves
description: Tubing through a cave lit with glowworms is a magical underground experience.

name: Milford Sound
description: Dolphins, ancient clams and hundreds of waterfalls make this one of the world's top tourist destinations.

name: Castle Hill
description: The Dalai Lama named this New Zealand landscape the "Spiritual Center of the Universe."

name: That Wanaka Tree
description: A solitary tree has grown up all alone on Lake Wānaka, backdropped by the beautiful Southern Alps.

name: The Tree Church
description: One man's dream has turned into this naturally growing chapel made of almost nothing but trees.

name: Devil's Bath (Roto Karikitea)
description: This electric green sulphur lake in New Zealand looks like a cartoonish radioactive dump site.

name: Takapō (Lake Tekapo)
description: A perfect turquoise lake colored with glacier dust.

name: Fox Glacier
description: A glacier pocked with ice caves and surrounded by rainforests.

name: Gibbs Farm
description: A gigantic sculpture garden is packed with innovative oddities.

name: Waipu Caves
description: A galaxy of glowworms—larvae of a gnat—hides deep in the belly of these caves.

name: Tunnel Beach
description: Known for the tunnel that leads visitors to the water.

name: Te Waikoropupu Springs
description: Some of the clearest water anywhere in the world.

name: Te Wairoa Buried Village
description: A Maori village obliterated by an 1886 volcanic eruption.

name: Kapiti Island Nature Reserve
description: Only 68 people per day are allowed to visit this New Zealand bird-watcher’s paradise.

name: Slope Point
description: The most southerly spot on New Zealand's South Island is home to surreally shaped, permanently mangled trees.

name: Rotorua
description: Major tourist destination known for its bubbling mud-pools and hot thermal springs.

name: Pancake Rocks
description: This strange geological landscape erupts with sudden bursts of water.

name: Baldwin Street  Houses
description: One of the world's steepest streets seems to have houses sliding down it.

name: Putangirua Pinnacles
description: These phallic stone spires once served as Tolkien's Paths of the Dead.

name: Colossal Squid
description: The world's only complete colossal squid specimen is on display at the Te Papa Museum.

name: Franz Josef Glacier
description: The glacier tucked within a New Zealand rainforest.

name: Electrum
description: One of the world's largest Tesla coils.

name: Tokangawhā / Split Apple Rock
description: This unusual rock formation looks like an apple that has been neatly sliced in half.

name: Frying Pan Lake
description: Deadly scalding and one of the largest hot springs in the world.

name: Taupō McDonald's Airplane
description: Eat your Big Mac in a DC-3.

name: Little Paradise Lodge
description: A lakeside garden in the Southern Alps full of whimsical artwork and thousands of beautiful flowers.

name: The Longest Place Name In The World
description:
Taumatawhakatangihangakoauauo 
tamateaturipukakapikimaungahoronuku 
pokaiwhenuakitanatahu.

name: Whakaari/White Island
description: The explorer Captain Cook may have named it White Island, but the Maori call it Te Puia o Whakaari – The Dramatic Volcano.

name: Rivendell
description: You may recognize this beautiful grove within Kaitoke Regional Park as location of the house of Elrond.

name: Hundertwasser Toilets
description: Modern art meets modern convenience in possibly the world's most architecturally important public bathroom.

name: Moeraki Boulders
description: Highly spherical boulders on the shores of New Zealand.

name: Lake Rotomahana
description: The once and future home of New Zealand's famed Pink and White Terraces.

name: Cape Reinga
description: Watch the seas clash at the place where according to Maori tradition, the spirits depart for their journey to the afterlife.

name: Puzzling World
description: New Zealand's epic shrine to all things puzzling.

name: Dark Sky Project
description: Home to some of the world’s darkest skies, this project blends ancient Māori culture with world-class astro tourism.

name: Steampunk HQ
description: The premier place to experience the Victorian imagining of the steam-powered future.

name: Hot Water Beach
description: Handcrafted thermal spa washes away each night with the tide.

name: Weta Cave
description: Inside this faux cave, visitors can learn how some of their favorite fantasy characters were created.

name: Tāne Mahuta
description: Largest Kauri tree in New Zealand.

name: Mrs. Chippy Monument
description: A bronze kitty adorns the grave of the polar explorer who brought the original feline to the Antarctic.

name: Dunedin Museum of Natural Mystery
description: Three rooms of bones, curios, and one artist's collection of natural artifacts create this intriguing museum.

name: Maungawhau (Mount Eden)
description: The platform atop this verdant volcano was built with the help of a royal elephant.

name: Wrights Hill Fortress
description: Anyone wanting to know what a dwarven mine sounds like need only visit the tunnels of this New Zealand artillery compound.

name: Mount Sunday
description: Edoras, capital city of Rohan in Lord of the Rings, once sat atop this isolated New Zealand mountain.

name: Rere Rockslide
description: Nature's slip and slide.

name: Larnach Castle
description: One of New Zealand’s few castles boasts beautiful gardens, grand architecture, and a history filled with family drama.

name: Muriwai Gannet Colony
description: New Zealand's west coast is one of the best places in the world to view these large seabirds.

name: World's Loneliest Tree
description: The most remote tree in the world is more than 170 miles from its nearest neighbor.

name: Wellington Cable Car
description: New Zealand's only running funicular railway.

name: Maori and Colonial Museum
description: One of the largest collections of traditional Māori artifacts in New Zealand.

name: Quasi
description: The 16-foot sculpture of a hand looks out from his perch on top of an art gallery.

name: Cardrona Bra Fence
description: Hundreds of bras, blowing in the wind.

name: Sky Tower
description: A New Zealand communications tower stands as the tallest structure in the Southern Hemisphere.

name: Demolition World
description: An eerily charming collection of salvaged buildings and curios grown out of a family scrap business.

name: Springfield Doughnut
description: A giant doughnut was given to the small New Zealand town of Springfield to promote "The Simpsons Movie."

name: Farewell Spit
description: This deadly little sliver of beach has seen hundreds of whales beach themselves over the years.

name: Browns Island
description: This quiet island in the middle of a bustling city is actually a volcano dotted with Māori ruins.

name: 'Diminish and Ascend'
description: This aluminum “stairway to heaven” has impaled at least a couple of birds.

name: The Lost Gypsy Gallery
description: This quirky collection is an interactive exhibit crammed with creative contraptions and tinkered-with toys.

name: Spacecraft Cemetery
description: The oceanic dumping ground where spaceships go to die.

name: Beverly Clock
description: Running since 1864, this clock has yet to be wound, one of the longest-running science experiments known.

name: Jackson's Rēwena Bread
description: This bakery sells traditional Māori sourdough.

name: Riff Raff Statue
description: The "Rocky Horror Picture Show" character has a hidden camera that broadcasts people doing the "Time Warp."

name: Big Dog and Sheep
description: These two delightful animal-shaped buildings host a visitor center and wool shop.

name: Arrowtown Chinese Settlement
description: This preserved village shows the modest lifestyles of the area's 19th-century Chinese gold miners.

name: Stonehenge Aotearoa
description: This modern New Zealand attraction honors ancient forms of astronomy and scientific understanding.

name: Fred and Myrtle's Paua Shell House
description: A New Zealand couple's shell-adorned home living room, now on display at the Canterbury Museum.

name: The Giants House
description: An artistic garden environment of tile mosaic sculptures.

name: Napier Prison
description: New Zealand's oldest prison holds a dark history and the scars of a deadly earthquake.

name: The Waterworks
description: Water-powered gadgets whirr to life in this quirky eco park.

name: Moniac Machine
description: A strange machine made to evaluate the world economy using water and lots of tubes.

name: Vanished World Centre
description: A hidden gem of a museum that lets you search for your own fossils.

name: The Alexander Piano
description: One of the world's largest pianos is displayed in the workshop of the man who built it as a teenager.

name: Te Pā Tū
description: An immersive Māori village where you can learn history and heritage through dance, ceremony, song, architecture, and food.

name: Abandoned WWII Structures of Motutapu Island
description: A network of fortifications that never saw conflict.

name: The Huddleston
description: A steampunk airship "floats" above Hamilton Gardens delivering plants and compost.

name: Te Paki Sand Dunes
description: Mounds of fine golden sand that can rise nearly 500 feet tall.

name: Bridge to Nowhere, Manawatu-Wanganui
description: This bridge was designed to connect a road that never came to fruition.

name: Old Bank Arcade Animated Musical Clock
description: A charmingly old-fashioned reminder of the historic building's former life.

name: Ward Beach Boulders
description: This beach provides a glimpse of the ancient seafloor without getting wet.

name: Hokonui Moonshine Museum
description: Celebrating the history of whiskey production during New Zealand's era of prohibition.

name: Stratford Glockenspiel Clock Tower
description: This mechanical clock performs "Romeo and Juliet" in a Shakespeare-themed New Zealand town.

name: Toothbrush Fence
description: Hundreds of toothbrushes hang from a wire fence along a quiet New Zealand road.

name: World of WearableArt & Classic Cars Museum
description: A journey through a collection of classic cars and fashionable artwork.

name: Sutton Salt Lake
description: New Zealand's only inland salt lake goes through a constant cycle of filling, evaporating, and refilling.

name: Driving Creek Railway
description: This incredible narrow-gauge mountain railway was created by a ceramic artist.

name: Symonds Street Cemetery
description: One of Auckland's oldest cemeteries was partially demolished during the construction of a major highway in the 1960s.

name: Eltham Toy Wall
description: What began as a makeshift lost and found, now exists as a unique art installation.

name: Jens Hansen Jewellery Workshop
description: The real place where the One Ring to rule them all was forged.

name: Koropuku Falls
description: The Catlins region has a bounty of breathtaking waterfalls, the best is arguably Koropuku Falls—a majestic waterfall with an enchanting bush walk.

name: Matakana Toilets
description: A serene, almost spiritual place to do one's business. Who knew a public toilet could inspire and awe?

name: Talking Poles
description: The wooden sculptures are studded throughout a small New Zealand town.

name: Zealandia
description: The world's first fully-fenced urban ecosanctuary is a haven for New Zealand's rare and endangered species.

name: The Town of Bulls
description: The "punniest place on Earth” has nearly as many bull-themed puns as it does people.

name: Creel Tackle House & Cafe
description: Come for the bait, stay for the brioche.

name: Boulder Bank
description: An unusual snaking bank of boulders caused by longshore drift.

name: Gumboot Capital of the World
description: Taihape's claim to fame is a fictional Kiwi farmer wearing gumboots.

name: Stony Batter Historic Reserve
description: Large World War II gun emplacements and tunnel systems remain hidden near a remote end of Waiheke Island.

name: Paddy the Wanderer Fountain
description: A memorial to Wellington's well-loved, well-traveled wharf dog.

name: Elephant Rocks
description: It took millions of years for these rocks to resemble their mammalian counterparts.

name: Mt. Cleese
description: A New Zealand rubbish dump named after British comedian John Cleese.

name: Mahurangi Harbour
description: A drowned river valley that was important to the Maori and European settlers.

name: Dance-O-Mat
description: A coin-operated washing machine turned jukebox, complete with an outdoor dance floor.

name: Letterbox Village
description: Leave a trinket at this artistic collection of mailboxes.

name: Ngātoro-i-rangi Carving at Mine Bay
description: This 46-foot-tall carving of a legendary Māori navigator can only be visited by boat.

name: Moai Statue
description: One of the iconic moai of Rapa Nui stands guard in Lyall Bay as a symbol of the friendship between New Zealand and Chile.

name: Natural Fizzy Spring
description: An unsigned roadside spring that is pure fun.

name: Hobbit Hideaway
description: This ledge in Victoria Park served as a hiding spot for Frodo, Sam, Merry and Pippin.

name: C1 Espresso
description: Get your meal delivered via pneumatic at this former post office.

name: Omarama Clay Cliffs
description: Towering earthen formations that rise straight out of the ground in New Zealand .

name: DEKA Sign
description: This faded sign to nowhere is a beloved icon in New Zealand.

name: Church of the Good Shepherd
description: This charming stone church on the shore of Lake Tekapo is an icon in astrophotography.

name: Forgotten World Highway
description: This remote highway took over 50 years to build.

name: Waikumete Cemetery
description: Home to the unidentified remains of the 1979 Mt. Erebus Disaster.

name: Wellington Airport Runway
description: High winds can make this one scary landing.

name: Salisbury Historic Swing-Bridge
description: New Zealand's most advertised nonexistent landmark

name: Kiwi North
description: A heritage park that explores the history and ecology of Whangārei.

name: Lobster Loos
description: These concrete crustaceans are actually public restrooms.

name: Ohakune Carrot
description: The largest model carrot in the world.

name: Listening and Viewing Device
description: This kinetic sculpture focuses the sounds of the city when you stand inside.

name: Benjamin the Library Cat
description: A bronze statue to commemorate an Auckland library's beloved feline resident.

name: Moko Tree
description: This 800-year-old Rimu tree may be the oldest of its kind in the world.

name: Bucket Fountain
description: Referred to as "the crappiest fountain ever," this sculpture has been splashing unknowing passersby since 1969.

name: Sam Summers Hut
description: This small stone hut was once home to a gold prospector and his family.

name: Ōnawe Peninsula
description: A peninsula on a peninsula that becomes an island at high tide.

name: Wharariki Beach
description: These stunning arched rock formations might be familiar to Windows 10 users.

name: Raurimu Rex
description: A life-sized statue of a Tyrannosaurus rex made out of driftwood.

name: 'The Lighthouse: Tū Whenua-a-Kura'
description: A neon-illuminated statue of James Cook at the edge of a historical Auckland wharf makes for an unexpected discovery.

name: Emerald Lakes (Ngarotopounamu)
description: These neon green and blue lakes are in the shadow of two huge volcanoes.

name: Napier Art Deco Historic Precinct
description: The highest concentration of Art Deco buildings in the world.

name: Wellington Writers Walk
description: Quotes and poems etched on stone plaques are hidden along this waterfront walkway.

name: Secret Slide
description: Wellington’s longest, fastest, and steepest slide offers incredible views of New Zealand's capital.

name: Cardboard Cathedral
description: This semi-permanent cathedral was built of cardboard, wood, and steel after Christchurch's 2011 earthquake.

name: Taranaki Wharf Jump Platform
description: A winding staircase that appears to lead to nowhere is actually a diving platform into the Wellington Harbour.

name: Flora and Otto
description: A mosaic armchair and ottoman built from china broken in the Christchurch earthquakes.

name: Toenga o Te Aro (Remains of Te Aro Pā)
description: The only known remains of an extensive Maori settlement are preserved underneath an apartment building.

name: Whanganui War Memorial Tower
description: A tower made from shell rock designed to commemorate fallen soldiers in World War I.

name: Te Puka (The Anchor Stone)
description: A giant chain sculpture that plays on the Māori myth that Rakiura (Stewart Island) is an anchor stone chained to the South Island.

name: Maungauika (North Head)
description: Walk through abandoned anti-Russian military tunnels on this volcano at the head of Auckland Harbor.

name: Nairn's Street Cottage
description: Journey through time by touring Wellington's oldest original cottage.

name: The Castle on Hawkin's Hill
description: A mysterious castle "in the clouds" has had many lives including a radar bunker, dog hotel, and conference center.

name: Asbestos Cottage
description: This backcountry hut was home to an asbestos miner and his wife for 40 years.

name: Plimmer's Ark Remains
description: The remnants of a 1849 sunken ship lie underneath a building.

name: E. Hayes & Sons
description: A quirky hardware store filled with museum-worthy cars and motorcycles, including a motorcycle that set a land speed record in 1967.

name: Ōtamahua Ship’s Graveyard
description: An uninhabited island in a New Zealand harbor with dozens of scuttled vessels.

name: The Puhoi Pub
description: Victorian-era pub covered head to toe with mementos from patrons of decades past.

name: Katherine Mansfield House
description: The "horrid little piggy house" of one of New Zealand's most celebrated writers.

name: Seacliff Asylum Ruins
description: The remains of a notorious mental institution that became known for its structural design failures and experiments carried out on patients.

name: Te Waha O Rerekohu
description: This great Pōhutukawa tree is believed to be the largest and oldest of its kind in the world.

name: Massey Memorial
description: The mausoleum of New Zealand's controversial prime minister.

name: National Transport & Toy Museum
description: Eclectic collection of cars, trucks, toys, aircraft, and machines from across the 20th and 21st centuries.

name: Bunsen
description: Enjoy coffee and cake while viewing antique medical equipment at this unique cafe.

name: Hiakai
description: This fine-dining restaurant features native New Zealand ingredients and Māori cooking.

name: Opossum World
description: This museum/fur store is devoted to educating guests on how awful opossums can be.

name: Christchurch Basilica
description: The ruins of New Zealand's largest classical-style church.

name: 185 Chairs
description: An unusual and powerful memorial in which earthquake victims are remembered by empty white chairs.

name: Lanyop Gallery
description: Free candle-lit art gallery, accompanied by a live piano score.

name: OnDo Giant Noodle Sculpture
description: In an Auckland neighborhood, looming chopsticks lift noodles from the sidewalk.

name: Re:START Mall
description: After the devastating 2011 earthquake, historic Christchurch begins to recover, starting with the pop-up Re:START mall.
